hypothesis: If a two-stage transfer learning approach that pretrains EfficientNet-B0 on a data-rich additive-free SEM dataset and fine-tunes it on a smaller dataset of additive-induced SEM images (with either oversampling-based cross-entropy or weighted cross-entropy to balance classes) is used, then classification performance (class-wise accuracy and F1) across the nine NCM composition–aging classes will exceed that of baseline training strategies without transfer learning, because pretraining transfers morphology-relevant features to the data-poor domain and mitigates class imbalance.
